summary: Validation confirms the recovered systemic interaction chain is legible at 1440x900 and 1280x720, with screenshot evidence, appointment/task/map synchronization assertions, and no unexplained console errors. This is validation evidence only and does not approve formal M7 content lock.

tests_run.result: Clean worktree after the validation commit and before writing the handoff.
Listed active tasks and confirmed the validation task is still the active systemic interaction validation work item.
Returned the current dependency-ready task context for this branch.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M7-SYSTEMIC-INTERACTION-VALIDATION-001

tests_run.result: No whitespace or patch formatting issues.
Format check, lint, workspace typecheck, and architecture boundary checks passed.
Web build completed successfully with chunk-size warnings only.
Playwright chromium suite passed: 29 tests, 29 passed.

tests_run.command: pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/web-shell.spec.ts -g "M7 systemic interaction validation captures required viewport evidence" --project=chromium
tests_run.result: 1 passed; generated the 1440x900 and 1280x720 evidence screenshots.

decisions.decision: The recovered first-screen, obligation, appointment, and decision-surface interactions are validated with console-clean evidence at both required viewport sizes.
This validation does not approve formal M7 content lock; it only supports returning M7-CONTENT-LOCK-ACCEPTANCE-001 to product-owner reconsideration after QA review.
No new gameplay mechanics, authoritative-state mutations, or forbidden package changes were introduced by this validation task.

risks: Formal M7 content lock remains a separate Human Gate and is not approved by this evidence package.
The smaller supported viewport is dense, but the required first-screen, decision, and feedback elements are visibly present and asserted.
requested_action: Review the validation evidence and route to lead_orchestrator if accepted. Do not approve formal M7 content lock from this handoff.
